one_time_description: Loss due to fire ($105,548) and impairment loss on portfolio investments ($368,484)
economic_notes: Fort Albany Power Corporation provides hydro-electric power distribution. 25% interest in Kimesskanemenow LP (winter road construction). 25% interest in Creewest LP (contracting opportunities). Minority interest in Mushkegowuk Development Corporation and Ontario First Nations Sovereign Wealth LP. COVID-19 impacted operations in early March 2020 with band office closures and program delivery changes.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of budget comparison figures in statements of operations and changes in net debt. Consolidated entities include Fort Albany First Nation Band, Mundo Peetabeck Education Authority, and Peetabeck Health Services. Fort Albany Power Corporation is accounted for using modified equity method (GBE). Large Impact Benefit Agreement receipt from De Beers was $350,200 in 2020 vs $6,860,014 in 2019 - significant one-year drop. Impairment loss on Kimesskanemenow LP investment of $368,484 recorded. Loss due to fire net of insurance proceeds. Tangible capital asset net book value not extractable from text provided (Schedule 1 not included in detail). COVID-19 noted as impacting operations. Sewage lagoon remediation liability of $600,000 accrued. ISC funding calculated as Flexible + Fixed + Block + Set + Grant = $16,594,188. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + FNIHB + Province of Ontario = $27,085,791 (excluding Mushkegowuk Council, NAN, Payukotayno as tribal/organizational transfers). OSR includes earnings from business entity, IBA receipts, winter road, rental income, other, net deferred revenue adjustments.
